title: DNA extraction from mouthwash samples using Qiagen DNeasy PowersoilPro Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1pdai","text":"This protocol describes how to extract DNA from mouthwash samples. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ebb1","text":"Set centrifuge to 4ºC","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c59dt","text":"Keep CD2 solution on ice or at 4ºC","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cinu2","text":"Prepare and label collection tubes, microcentrifuge tubes, MB spin columns, and powerbead tubes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"424a6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e8mi6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7dgt","text":"QIAGEN -DNeasy PowerSoil Pro Kit ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c41to","text":"Ice","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6vlnr","text":"Vortex","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ilbb","text":"Omni Bead Ruptor Elite bead beater","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvg4p","text":"Centrifuge ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"euq48","text":"Liquid N2 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":8,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7n73g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw the sample on ice for 00:30:00
Centrifuge the transferred sample at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) at 4°C for 00:10:00
Transfer the desired sample volume 1-2 mL into a 1.5mL or 2mL eppendorf tube
Discard the supernatant carefully without disturbing the pellet
Repeat Step 1.1 if more volume is needed to see a pellet or a higher yield of DNA is required
Add 800 µL of CD1 and vortex to resuspend pellet
Spin down briefly
Transfer entire eppendorf content in a PowerBead Pro Tube
Secure PowerBead Pro tube onto the bead beater and run at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:05:00
Centrifuge the PowerBead Pro tube at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:01:30
Transfer the supernatant carefully, without disturbing the pellet, into a clean 2mL microcentrifuge tube
Add 200 µL of CD2 into the 2mL microcentrifuge tube and vortex for 00:00:10
Centrifuge the 2mL microcentrifuge tube at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:01:30
Transfer the supernatant carefully without disturbing the pellet, into a clean 2mL microcentrifuge tube
Add 600 µL of CD3 into the 2 mL microcentrifuge tube and vortex for 00:00:10
Load 650 µL of the lysate onto MB spin columns and centrifuge at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:01:30
Discard flow-through and repeat step 13 to consume all the lysate
Place the spin column onto a new collection tube, add 500 µL of EA, and centrifuge at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:01:30
Discard flow-through and place the spin column back into the collection tube
Add 500 µL of C5 onto the spin column and centrifuge at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:01:30
Discard flow-through and place the spin column back into the collection tube
Centrifuge at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:02:00 and place spin column into 1.5mL eppendorf (elution) tube
Add 50-100 µL of nuclease-free water to the center of the spin column and leave at room temperature for 00:05:00
Centrifuge at maximum speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ) for 00:01:00 , quantify using Qubit, flash freeze, and store at -20/80 °C
creator: Genetic Heritage Group NYU Abu Dhabi
